name: Pumpkin Marble Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the gingersnap crumbs, pecans, and melted margarine in a bowl. Press this mixture into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan to form a crust.
2. Bake at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 10 minutes or until lightly golden.
3. In another bowl, beat together the cream cheese, sugar, and vanilla extract with an electric mixer on medium speed until well blended.
4. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing thoroughly after each addition.
5. Set aside half of this batter to chill while preparing the remaining layers.
6. To the chilled batter, add the remaining sugar, pumpkin puree, and ground nutmeg. Mix well to combine.
7. Using a knife or spatula, alternate spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture and the chilled cream cheese batter over the crust in the springform pan.
8. Cut through the layered batter several times with a knife to create a marble effect.
9. Bake at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 55 minutes, or until set but slightly jiggly in the center when gently tapped.
10. Remove from the oven and let cool completely before serving. Once cooled, refrigerate the cheesecake until ready to serve.
